title: Use nameof for parameter names in argument exceptions
summary: Passing a parameter name to an argument exception as a string literal silently rots when the parameter is renamed: the refactoring tool updates the signature but leaves the literal pointing at a name that no longer exists. The nameof operator binds the text to the symbol, so a rename keeps them in step and a typo becomes a compile error.
messageFormat: Use 'nameof' instead of a string literal to refer to the parameter.
